description:
You are a system administrator preparing for scheduled disk maintenance. Before proceeding, you need to back up the volume header of a critical encrypted container so that the data can be recovered if the header gets corrupted.

Using VeraCrypt, create a backup of the volume header for the following encrypted container:

- Volume path: /home/ga/Volumes/data_volume.hc
- Volume password: MountMe2024
- PIM: 0 (default)
- Keyfiles: None

Save the header backup file to:
- /home/ga/Volumes/data_volume_header_backup.bin

When prompted about whether to create a backup of the hidden volume header, choose No (there is no hidden volume in this container).

The original volume should remain intact and unmounted after the operation. You may use either the VeraCrypt GUI (Tools > Backup Volume Header) or the VeraCrypt CLI to accomplish this task.
